typecheck.summary: Success: no issues found in 91 source files

appliedUpdates.description: Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements, so component-level testing with Python mocks is the established project convention for 'E2E' tests. This is documented in test files but not in any central reference.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The e2e-worker skill should document the project-specific constraint that Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements, so 'E2E' tests use component-level testing with Python mocks rather than Playwright browser automation. The skill should be updated to reflect this project convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test_session_page.py, test_home_page.py, test_login_screen.py all use the same component-level testing pattern with Python mocks, explicitly noting this is because 'Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements.'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for naming test classes to match validation contract assertion numbers. The current convention (TestSessionPageTTSButtons naming but VAL-SESSION-009 content) creates cross-reference confusion.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-session-page review found class names like TestSessionPageTTSButtons claim VAL-SESSION-004 but actually test TTS functionality (VAL-SESSION-009).

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker used mismatched test class names that don't match VAL-SESSION numbering
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a non-blocking convention issue. The test METHOD names correctly match the validation contract. The class name mismatch is organizational confusion, not a functional bug. The suggested fix (add guidance for class naming) is captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates.
